q: How many days of unemployment am I allowed on post-completion OPT?
expected: Up to 90 days of unemployment during post-completion OPT (150 total with the STEM extension).

q: Can I start working before my EAD start date?
expected: No. You may not begin OPT until the start date on your EAD.

q: When can I apply for post-completion OPT?
expected: Up to 90 days before your program end date and no later than 60 days after; within 30 days of the DSO's SEVIS recommendation.

q: Does part-time work count toward maintaining OPT status?
expected: Post-completion OPT must be at least 20 hours/week; below that you are considered unemployed.

q: How long is the grace period after my OPT ends?
expected: 60 days after the EAD expiration date.

q: How long is the STEM OPT extension, and who's eligible?
expected: 24 months, for students with a qualifying STEM degree, in valid post-completion OPT, with an E-Verify employer.

q: Do I need a job offer to apply for OPT?
expected: No job offer is required to apply for post-completion OPT.

q: How soon must I report a change of employer?
expected: Report changes (incl. employer) to your DSO within 10 days.

q: Can I travel internationally while on OPT?
expected: Generally yes with valid documents; specifics depend on EAD/job offer and cap-gap status. Confirm with DSO.
Automatic extension of F-1 status/OPT bridging the gap to an Oct 1 H-1B start for timely-filed cap-subject petitions.

q: Can I do volunteer or unpaid work to avoid unemployment days?
expected: Volunteer/unpaid work related to your field, at least 20 hrs/week and not violating labor laws, may count.
Self-employment can qualify on post-completion OPT if the business is related to the field and properly run; not allowed for STEM OPT.

q: What happens if my program ends while my OPT application is still pending?
expected: Post-completion OPT can be pending after program end; you may not work until the EAD is approved and its start date arrives.

q: Can I work for more than one employer on OPT?
expected: Yes on regular post-completion OPT (each job related to your field); STEM OPT requires bona fide employer-employee relationships and E-Verify.

q: Does my OPT job have to relate to my major?
expected: Yes. All OPT employment must be directly related to your major area of study.
